raw_consumer_statement:
Subject : Credit Card Dispute Handling Error Resulting in Loss of Refund Complaint : I am filing a complaint regarding a billing error and failure to properly process a valid refund related to a returned purchase. 

Purchase Details : Laptop with XXXX for {$430.00} Order Placed XX/XX/year>. 

I purchased a laptop from XXXX using my Citi Double Cash credit card. When I initially attempted to return the item, XXXX failed to provide a return label in a timely manner, so I filed a dispute with my credit card issuer. 

After the dispute was filed, XXXX provided a return label, and I promptly returned the laptop. Tracking confirms the item was successfully delivered back to XXXX. 

Because the return was completed, I closed the dispute. However, after I closed the dispute, XXXX issued a refund through the chargeback/dispute system instead of processing a standard refund. 

Because the dispute had already been closed, my credit card issuer rejected the refund, and the funds were returned to XXXX. As a result, I no longer have the laptop and have not received my refund. 

I have contacted my credit card issuer multiple times to resolve this issue. They have refused to reopen the dispute, reprocess the refund, or provide a clear resolution. They continue to direct me back to the merchant, despite the fact that the refund failure was caused by the timing and handling of the chargeback process. 

This situation constitutes a billing error under the Fair Credit Billing Act, as I was charged for merchandise that I returned and no longer possess.
